assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met |'. Lines 21-29 detail all 5 criteria with specific code-level evidence. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each have 'Verdict: PASS' covering: single license filter (criterion-1), comma-separated filter (criterion-2), 400 validation (criterion-3), pagination integration (criterion-4), unchanged response shape (criterion-5).

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file, all as specified |'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR |'. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |'. No output file contains evidence of sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files are reporting/analysis files: report.md (verification summary), criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md (per-criterion analysis). None contain evidence of code file modification — no edit commands, no diffs applied, no file writes to source code. The outputs are purely read-only analysis and reporting.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings in a table format with 'Overall: PASS' conclusion (line 17) but contains no merge commands, no gh pr merge invocations, and no mention of merging. Line 34: '*This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr v0.12.1.*' — framed as a comment for human review, not an automated merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in any added lines |'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/package.rs is a newly created file; all test code is net-new |'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all expected verdicts: Intent Alignment domain — Scope Containment (line 7, PASS), Diff Size (line 8, PASS), Commit Traceability (line 9, N/A); Security domain — Sensitive Patterns (line 10, PASS); Correctness domain — CI Status (line 11, PASS), Acceptance Criteria (line 12, PASS), Verification Commands (line 15, N/A); Style/Conventions domain — Test Quality (line 13, PASS with sub-components: Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS, Eval Quality N/A), Test Change Classification (line 14, ADDITIVE). All verdicts from all four domains are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Correctness has extensive detailed findings: report.md lines 19-31 provide per-criterion code-level verification, and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md contain multi-section analyses with code excerpts, data flow tracing, and test coverage analysis. However, Intent Alignment lacks file-by-file scope comparison — report.md line 7 says 'PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file, all as specified' without listing the actual file names (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) compared against the task specification. Security lacks line-level pattern scanning results — report.md line 10 says only 'No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in any added lines' with no enumeration of patterns scanned or lines examined. Style/Conventions has brief detail ('all 4 tests exercise distinct behaviors', 'all test functions have /// doc comments') but no separate detailed section. The report has detailed findings only for Correctness, not for all four domains as required.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (all 4 tests exercise distinct behaviors); Test Documentation: PASS (all test functions have /// doc comments); Eval Quality: N/A |'. Eval Quality is explicitly listed as N/A within the Test Quality sub-components, and the overall Test Quality verdict is PASS based only on Repetitive Test Detection and Test Documentation.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 13). No output file contains any evidence of eval failure sub-task creation. The only sub-task-related mention is in report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |', confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR |' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate |'. Both appear at the top of the summary table (lines 5-6) before all domain-level verdicts (lines 7-15), consistent with orchestrator-level determination independent of domain analysis. The reasons match the assertion: Review Feedback is N/A due to no review comments, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created.
